text: he said, unable to articulate his more complex thoughts . “That’s putting it mildly.” She chuckled . “I’m sorry. Are you going to apologize to me , now ?” “ I have nothing to say sorry for.” When he opened his mouth to reply, she cut him off. “I missed . And you could’ve just called my name and avoided all of that, instead of scaring the shit outta me .” “Shit. Quite a versatile word… I am sorry, Lara Brooks. This was not my intention .” “I know.” She pulled back and looked him over, the humor vanishing from her face. “What can I do to help ?” “Not much. The Clinic is the only place that can repair this damage. You can help get some of the shrapnel out, and remove the damaged skin. At least then we can trade some of it to the scrapper and make a bit of profit from this .” “That’s morbid .” “I am more than the sum of my parts,” he said, and she tilted her head. Another thought chain he could not adequately explain. “It’s practical. If they are removed at the Clinic, they’d likely keep the material to reuse without offering compensation .” He released his embrace and put a hand on the railing, stabilizing himself. With his single optic, he looked up to the second floor. He’d have to handle it just like he had the stairs in that basement; slowly, one step at a time . “I moved my tools into my pack. Bring them upstairs. I’ll meet you there .” “ Okay .” Ronin waited for her to go up first, calculating the journey ahead. His upward climb was slow, but rhythmic — the creak of the bannister, the groan of a step, the thump of his damaged leg. Over and over, until he finally reached the top, one minute and twenty-two seconds later . He kept his head angled down as he walked toward his bedroom, staring at the metal planes of his chest and abdomen, at the charred clothing and melted skin. Ronin bore little resemblance to a human, currently, but she’d thrown her arms around him and showed concern for his state. She wouldn’t have done the same, that first night . They’d both changed so much in so short a time . Lara had the tools spread out on the chest when he entered the room. Some of them were smudged with soot or singed, grips warped from the heat of the flames . “Lie down and tell me what to do .” He moved to the bed, right leg dragging despite his best efforts. The bedding was rumpled, the blankets tossed aside. Had she been sleeping here when came home ? The springs creaked as he sat, and he recalled the many sounds the bed had made during more pleasant activities. He swung his left leg up without trouble. Lara only allowed him a moment’s struggle with the right before she took hold of his ankle and helped him lift it . “Lie back.” She pressed her palms to his chest and guided him down onto the mattress . “You’ll need to pull out the shrapnel. Use the pliers .” Lara went to the chest, selecting a pair of pliers, and returned to the bedside. Catching her lower lip between her teeth, she looked over Ronin. Without a word, she climbed atop him, swinging a leg over his hips to straddle his abdomen, her back facing him. The hem of her shirt slid up her thighs, bare skin warm against his casing . She looked over her shoulder. “So…just pull them out? It won’t hurt you, right? Won’t do any more damage ?” “I’ll be fine,” he said. “Just take out whatever you can get to.” Her heat pulsed through his pants, and her position… He focused his attention on the points of damage. This wasn’t a sexual act; she was simply assisting in the first step toward repairing his casing . Turning away, she muttered to herself. “Okay, I can do this.” She leaned forward and tore his pants open around his knee. There was no pain when she worked the pliers into the hole, only pressure and the sound of metal scraping metal . “In there good.” She shifted atop him, and if he had skin… “Think I got it .” Lara leaned back, and Ronin’s internal sensors registered the force she exerted on the shard in his knee. Electric sparked up his leg . The pliers’ teeth snapped together suddenly, and Lara jerked back. He placed his hands on her sides to steady her, but her torso fell atop his, her hair covering his face. His olfactory sensors detected its fresh, clean scent. He lamented that his bare face plates weren’t sensitive enough to feel the delicate brush of the strands . At least he could feel her weight and the pleasurable warmth she emitted . “I almost got it .” He dropped his hands to her hips and helped her sit up. She pressed the pliers into the hole, but Ronin’s focus moved to her bare legs, her thighs squeezing his abdomen; he was her anchor. Given the situation, it seemed an odd place for his attention . Why was the play of her leg muscles so fascinating, so alluring? Why did her touch mute the concerns that should’ve been the foremost of his processes? He should have gone directly to the Clinic when he arrived in Cheyenne, should have received the necessary repairs before returning to her. Should have spared Lara all this worry . Thoughts of her had carried him back over the uneven landscape. His worry had not been for himself or the damage he’d sustained, but for her. How might she have reacted to Ronin’s deactivation ? It was a foolish thought-chain to pursue; realistically, the chances of her having ever learned of his demise were extremely small. But he wondered, anyway —
